A1.label: Is the proposed solution an AI system according to the EU AI Act?
A1.info: First, it is assessed whether the proposed solution meets the definition of an AI system. Only then do additional EU AI Act requirements become relevant.
examples: A machine learning model that automatically rates credit scores.
A static, rule-based system without learning or inference logic.
A third-party LLM is used in a critical process.

AI_ACT_NICHT_ZUTREFFEND.label: EU AI Act: Not applicable – baseline notes

KI_NZ_KLASSIFIZIERUNGSHINWEIS.question: Is it documented why the system does not qualify as an AI system under the EU AI Act (definition/boundary)?
KI_NZ_KLASSIFIZIERUNGSHINWEIS.todo: Document why the system does not qualify as an AI system under the EU AI Act (scope, system description, boundary).
KI_NZ_KLASSIFIZIERUNGSHINWEIS.info: The rationale should clearly explain why the definition in Art. 3 EU AI Act is not met.
examples: A note explains the distinction from rule-based decision tables without a model.
The system description lists inputs/outputs and explains which AI definition elements are missing.
